% Figure: Tree → 3D Graph (Kontablo) → Tree  (VERTICAL, top-to-bottom)
% Concept: Local ERP flat 2D tree (top) is imported into Kontablo's
%          multi-dimensional 3D graph (middle); the graph is linearized
%          back into a flat 2D IFRS output tree (bottom).
% Rendering: generated by figures/fig_tree_graph_tree.py (matplotlib +
%          networkx, real 3D middle layer). To regenerate the PDF:
%              cd docs/papers/drafts && python3 figures/fig_tree_graph_tree.py
%          The old hand-built TikZ version is preserved (commented) at the
%          bottom of this file as an audit-trail fallback.
\begin{figure}[p]
\centering
\includegraphics[height=0.92\textheight,keepaspectratio]{figures/fig_tree_graph_tree.pdf}
\caption{Tree-to-Graph-to-Tree: the Kontablo linearization protocol, read
top to bottom. A local ERP chart of accounts (top, orange) is a rigid 2-D
tree in which each account has a single parent. On import (Tree-to-Graph), it
enters the Kontablo Universal Graph (middle, blue), where each account node
carries a UUID and participates simultaneously in multiple independent graph
dimensions --- IFRS/Balance Sheet (solid blue), Tax/Regulatory (dashed red),
and Functional/Cost-Centre (dotted violet); the middle layer is drawn in 3-D
to make this multi-dimensionality explicit. For reporting or ERP export, the
graph is linearized (Graph-to-Tree) by selecting a single dimension as the
primary axis, producing a clean 2-D IFRS output tree (bottom, green) without
loss of the multi-dimensional data held in the graph.}
\label{fig:tree_graph_tree}
\end{figure}
